text:
Once upon a time, in a tiny village wrapped around a great forest, there lived an old owl named Oliver. Oliver was not your average owl. His feathers had an odd, quilted pattern that seemed to gleam with a soft, silver light in the moonlight.

Every day, Oliver would fly out of his nest high up in the gnarled branches of an ancient oak and across the meadow. He loved exploring. One day, after sunset, he spotted a peculiar glittering object amidst the wild flowers. Intrigued, he flew closer.

It was a crystal ball, slightly tarnished but still shining. Oliver inspected it keenly. Its surface crackled with ancient magic. He thought perhaps it had been a gift from a distant land. That night, as stars danced in the azure sky, he whispered a wish into it.

"Tell me the secrets of the woods," Oliver vowed.

A soft voice answered, gentle and wise: "The path is hard, the forest
